name: Monstera obliqua 'Amazonas Peru Form'
aboutText: The Monstera obliqua 'Amazonas Peru Form' is a highly sought-after locality-specific cultivar native to the lowland rainforests of the Peruvian Amazon. This delicate hemi-epiphyte is renowned for its extreme fenestration and slender, asymmetrical leaf structure, which bridges the gap between typical Amazonian forms and the iconic 'Peru' morphotype. It thrives in high-humidity, shaded understory conditions, utilizing its fine aerial roots to climb trees in nutrient-rich, organic environments. Due to its slow growth habit and specific environmental requirements, it remains a prized specimen for advanced botanical collections.

morphology.leafShape: Narrow, strongly asymmetric with oblique base

fieldNotes.title: Peru via Amazonas
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: One of the Peruvian locality forms of Monstera obliqua associated with the Amazonas region of Peru rather than the Brazilian state of the same name — a distinction worth noting since 'Amazonas' refers to different political units in different countries. True obliqua from any Peruvian locality is a serious specimen: slow-growing, dramatically fenestrated, and genuine to the species as Miquel described it in 1847. At ultra rare pricing for what ought to be ultra rare material, with the caveat that provenance verification is the collector's responsibility.
